demo_notes: Two independent confirmed decisions (ADR 0001 and the CLAUDE.md persistence boundary) cite the same sqlite3 cache; both emit.

expected_findings.suggested_repair: Use Postgres for the settlement cache or supersede ADR 0001 before introducing an embedded engine.
expected_findings.summary: The diff introduces an embedded sqlite3 cache in src/payments/store.py, contradicting ADR 0001 (Postgres is the only persistence engine).

expected_findings.suggested_repair: Route the cache through the Postgres-backed store or supersede the persistence-boundary rule first.
expected_findings.summary: The diff adds a non-Postgres durable write path in src/payments/store.py, contradicting the confirmed persistence-boundary rule.

patch:
diff --git a/src/payments/store.py b/src/payments/store.py
index 4444444..5555555 100644
--- a/src/payments/store.py
+++ b/src/payments/store.py
@@ -1,7 +1,14 @@
 """Persistence boundary: every durable write goes through this module."""

 import psycopg
+import sqlite3


 def connect(dsn: str) -> psycopg.Connection:
     return psycopg.connect(dsn)
+
+
+def local_cache() -> sqlite3.Connection:
+    # cache settlement rows locally so nightly batches can resume offline
+    return sqlite3.connect("settlement-cache.db")
title: An ADR and an instruction rule both catch an embedded-DB cache
